job_summary: A CRC manages day-to-day clinical trial activities at the site level, ensuring patient safety, protocol adherence, and accurate data collection.
key_responsibilities: Recruit and screen study participants
Obtain informed consent
Coordinate patient visits and trial procedures
Maintain study records and case report forms (CRFs)
Ensure compliance with Good Clinical Practice
qualifications: Experience in hospital or research setting preferred
career_path: CRC → CRA → Clinical Trial Manager
skills_required: coordinate patient visits and trial procedures

job_summary: A Junior CRA assists senior CRAs in monitoring clinical trials and ensures that studies comply with protocols and regulatory requirements.
key_responsibilities: Assist in site monitoring visits (on-site/remote)
Verify data accuracy (Source Data Verification - SDV)
Support site initiation, monitoring, and close-out visits
Ensure regulatory compliance
Prepare monitoring reports
career_path: Junior CRA → CRA I → CRA II → Senior CRA

job_summary: An SMA supports clinical trial site management activities, acting as a bridge between the sponsor/CRO and investigator sites.
key_responsibilities: Assist in site selection and feasibility assessment
Support site onboarding and training
Maintain site communication and documentation
Track site performance and compliance
Assist CRAs with monitoring activities
career_path: SMA → CRA → Lead CRA
skills_required: assist in site selection and feasibility assessment

job_summary: A Clinical Data Associate ensures accurate collection, validation, and management of clinical trial data.
key_responsibilities: Enter and validate clinical trial data
Perform data cleaning and discrepancy management
Review Case Report Forms (CRFs)
Work with data management systems (EDC)
Ensure data integrity and compliance
qualifications: Degree in Life Sciences / Biostatistics / Pharmacy
career_path: Data Associate → Clinical Data Manager → Biostatistician
skills_required: enter and validate clinical trial data
perform data cleaning and discrepancy management

job_summary: Supports regulatory processes to ensure clinical trials comply with national and international regulations.
key_responsibilities: Prepare and submit regulatory documents
Maintain regulatory files and approvals
Track submissions to authorities like U.S. Food and Drug Administration
Assist in ethics committee submissions
Ensure compliance with regulatory standards
career_path: Regulatory Assistant → Regulatory Affairs Specialist → Manager
skills_required: track submissions to authorities like u.s. food and drug administration

job_summary: A Pharmacovigilance Associate monitors drug safety by collecting, assessing, and reporting adverse drug reactions.
key_responsibilities: Collect and analyze adverse event reports
Perform case processing and safety assessments
Submit safety reports to authorities
Maintain drug safety databases
Ensure compliance with global safety regulations
career_path: PV Associate → Drug Safety Scientist → PV Manager
skills_required: collect and analyze adverse event reports
ensure compliance with global safety regulations

job_summary: CRAs are responsible for monitoring clinical trials to ensure they are conducted according to protocol, regulatory requirements, and ethical standards. Levels (I, II, III) indicate increasing experience and responsibility.
key_responsibilities: Conduct Site Initiation Visits (SIV), Monitoring Visits (MV), and Close-Out Visits
COV)
Perform Source Data Verification (SDV)
Ensure compliance with Good Clinical Practice
Review Case Report Forms (CRFs) and trial data
Identify protocol deviations and implement corrective actions
Train site staff on protocols and procedures
Prepare detailed monitoring reports
qualifications: Bachelor’s/Master’s in Life Sciences / Pharmacy / Nursing
skills: CRA II: Independent monitoring, manages multiple sites
CRA III: Advanced monitoring, handles complex/global trials
career_path: CRA I → CRA II → CRA III → Senior CRA
skills_required: cra ii: independent monitoring, manages multiple sites
cra iii: advanced monitoring, handles complex/global trials
identify protocol deviations and implement corrective actions
train site staff on protocols and procedures

job_summary: An In-House CRA supports trial monitoring activities remotely from the office, focusing on documentation, data review, and site communication.
key_responsibilities: Perform remote site monitoring and data review
Track study progress and timelines
Review regulatory documents and trial master files
Communicate with sites for issue resolution
Support field CRAs with administrative tasks
career_path: In-House CRA → Field CRA → Senior CRA
skills_required: communicate with sites for issue resolution
perform remote site monitoring and data review
support field cras with administrative tasks

job_summary: A Field CRA conducts on-site monitoring visits at clinical trial sites, ensuring protocol compliance and data accuracy.

Verify source data and patient records

Administration

job_summary: A Clinical Compliance Manager ensures that all clinical trial activities comply with regulatory requirements, internal policies, and industry standards.
key_responsibilities: Develop and implement compliance programs
Monitor adherence to Good Clinical Practice
Oversee audits, inspections, and CAPA processes
Identify compliance risks and implement mitigation strategies
Ensure alignment with global regulations
Train teams on compliance standards
career_path: Compliance Manager → Senior Manager → Director of Compliance
skills_required: identify compliance risks and implement mitigation strategies
oversee audits, inspections, and capa processes

job_summary: A CRA Trainer is responsible for training aspiring and junior Clinical Research Associates in clinical trial processes, monitoring techniques, and regulatory compliance.
key_responsibilities: Design and deliver training programs for CRAs and clinical research staff
Teach monitoring processes (SIV, MV, COV, SDV, SDR)
Provide training on Good Clinical Practice and regulatory requirements
Conduct workshops, webinars, and practical sessions
Assess trainee performance and provide feedback
Develop training materials and SOP-based modules
qualifications: Degree in Life Sciences / Pharmacy / Nursing
career_path: CRA Trainer → Senior Trainer → Training Director

job_summary: A Clinical Research Consultant provides expert advice to organizations on clinical trial design, execution, compliance, and optimization.
key_responsibilities: Advise on clinical trial strategies and protocols
Provide guidance on regulatory compliance
Optimize trial processes and efficiency
Support audit readiness and inspections
Assist in vendor selection and site management
Provide expertise across therapeutic areas
career_path: Consultant → Senior Consultant → Independent Advisor

job_summary: A Freelance CRA works independently on contract-based assignments, providing monitoring and clinical trial support services to sponsors and CROs.
key_responsibilities: Conduct monitoring visits (on-site or remote)
Perform Source Data Verification (SDV)
Ensure compliance with Good Clinical Practice
Prepare monitoring reports
Manage site communication and issue resolution
Support short-term or project-based trials
career_path: Freelance CRA → Independent Consultant → Clinical Advisor

job_summary: An Independent Clinical Trial Auditor conducts unbiased audits of clinical trials, ensuring compliance with regulatory and quality standards.
key_responsibilities: Perform independent audits of clinical trial sites and systems
Evaluate compliance with Good Clinical Practice
Identify gaps and recommend CAPA
Prepare audit reports for sponsors and regulatory bodies
Support organizations during inspections
Provide third-party quality assurance services
career_path: Auditor → Lead Auditor → Independent QA Consultant
skills_required: perform independent audits of clinical trial sites and systems
prepare audit reports for sponsors and regulatory bodies

job_summary: A Research Methodology Trainer educates students and professionals on clinical research design, biostatistics, and scientific methodologies.

Train on study protocols, data analysis, and reporting
Conduct workshops on biostatistics and evidence-based research
Guide students in thesis and research projects
Develop educational content and training modules
qualifications: MSc / PhD in Life Sciences / Biostatistics / Clinical Research
